rationales.self_direction: In the response he says the afternoon felt different because it was chosen rather than scheduled, framing the early exit as time that was finally his.
rationales.hedonism: He turns the broken Friday into pleasure for its own sake by lingering over pho and lychee tea, staying forty extra minutes, and savoring the rainy walk home instead of doing anything productive.
Diego's mango story lands because Santiago decides the good parts of life are specific textures and pleasures, and he wants to keep collecting those moments.
rationales.achievement: He explicitly rejects promotions, salary bumps, and resume lines as the real point of life, treating achievement markers as a bad trade for lived experience.

rationales.self_direction: He resists his mother's comparison frame and ends by insisting, 'I'm not lost. I just have a different map,' grounding his life in his own definition of success.
rationales.achievement: He treats Eduardo's promotion, apartment, and twelve-hour grind as 'not winning,' explicitly rejecting advancement and career racing as his metric.
rationales.conformity: On the call he withholds that view, says 'that's great for Eduardo,' and changes the subject instead of openly pushing back on his mother's expectations.

rationales.hedonism: After a punishing week, the part he elevates as worth everything is the tinto, balcony, parrots, and quiet morning hour that felt restorative in itself.
He deliberately leaves at five, buys the earbuds he has wanted for months, and immerses himself in the empanada, music, and bass as a pocket of pleasure.
rationales.conformity: The enjoyment is immediately policed by comparison to Diego and the juniors still at their desks, and in the response he ties that guilt to his father's rule that rest must be earned.

rationales.hedonism: Rest would have helped, but he denies himself both sleep recovery and a sick day, sitting through meetings with a headache after only three hours of sleep.
rationales.conformity: He goes to work despite being unwell because staying home would also feel guilty, showing deference to the expectation that he should still show up.

rationales.hedonism: He spends the afternoon eating, joking, and wandering by the river, and says he did not want to be anywhere else in that moment.
rationales.security: In the response he admits Andrés is part of the routine that keeps weekends from feeling empty, so the possible move matters partly because it threatens a familiar structure.

rationales.hedonism: He keeps planning Nuqui, chooses the nicer hostel with a hammock area, and is still reaching for the waves and vacation pleasure underneath the guilt.
rationales.conformity: The whole planning session is shadowed by rehearsed justifications about backlog, API docs, and on-call coverage, showing how strongly workplace expectations are policing his leisure.
